channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Green MP Hannah Spencer discusses her unexpected victory, working-class roots and the challenges of political authenticity
item.description: Hannah Spencer, a 34‑year‑old plumber from Bolton, recently became the Green party’s fifth sitting MP after winning the Gorton and Denton byelection in Greater Manchester with a remarkable 26% swing f...

item.title: Eighth Immortal Andrew Johns urges Raiders up-and-comer Ethan Strange to adjust carry hand for better impact
item.description: Andrew Johns, revered as one of rugby league’s greatest halfbacks, has nudged Canberra Raiders youngster Ethan Strange to refine his carry technique. Johns observed that Strange predominantly carries ...

item.title: Kerala Elections 2026: Can Vijayan withstand anti-incumbency and rebels backed by the UDF?
item.description: Kerala has long followed a pattern of power alternating between two coalitions—the LDF led by Pinarayi Vijayan and the UDF led by the Congress. Vijayan’s LDF bucked this trend in 2021 by returning to ...

item.title: Fear drives markets faster than fundamentals: balancing risk and opportunity in a volatile landscape
item.description: This piece discusses how market sentiment can outpace underlying fundamentals, amplifying volatility across equities and sectors. It highlights that fears—ranging from artificial intelligence disrupti...

item.title: Stats SA reports data breach affecting jobseekers’ online applications
item.description: Stats SA has confirmed a cybersecurity incident that affected its human resources database, particularly the online system used by jobseekers to submit applications. The breach was identified over the...

item.title: Report warns of systematic erosion of rule of law in five EU countries
item.description: A report by the Civil Liberties Union for Europe (Liberties) has identified five EU governments — Bulgaria, Croatia, Hungary, Italy and Slovakia — as systematically undermining the rule of law. The wa...

item.title: NCC issues compliance notices to 62 non-compliant stores across South Africa
item.description: The National Consumer Commission (NCC) has issued compliance notices to 62 businesses across South Africa after uncovering widespread violations of the Consumer Protection Act during a series of inspe...

item.title: PDP National Convention Proceeds as Party Grapples with Internal Divisions
item.description: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) recently held its national convention, backed by a faction led by former Rivers State Governor Nyesom Wike, amidst ongoing internal crises. The convention aimed to e...

item.title: Specialist kennels overwhelmed as XL bully attacks rise despite UK ban
item.description: Following the 2024 UK-wide ban on XL bully dogs, private specialist kennels have become critical holding facilities for animals seized after violent incidents. BBC Panorama gained rare access to one s...

item.title: Atlassian faces potential US shareholder class action amid AI fears and stock slump
item.description: Atlassian, the Sydney-based software company, could face a US shareholder class action as law firms seek to recruit investors who incurred losses during a sharp decline in Atlassian shares. The slide ...

item.title: Gayton McKenzie threatens legal action after inmate Jermaine Prim’s allegations surface in media and parliamentary scrutiny
item.description: The Department of Correctional Services (DCS) is investigating comments made by Jermaine Prim during an eNCA telephone interview, in which Prim alleged connections between Gayton McKenzie, leader of t...

item.title: South Africa Faces Record Petrol and Diesel Price Increases from April
item.description: South Africa is bracing for some of the steepest fuel price increases in recent memory, with petrol expected to rise by more than R5 per litre and diesel by over R10 from Wednesday, 1 April 2026. Acco...

item.title: Stormers rally in second half to secure vital URC win over Edinburgh
item.description: Stormers director of rugby John Dobson confessed to feeling uneasy when his side fell behind 14–12 against Edinburgh in their United Rugby Championship encounter at the DHL Stadium. Despite a sloppy p...

item.title: Nestlé reports KitKat theft: over 12 tonnes of chocolate stolen en route to Europe
item.description: Nestlé has issued an update on KitKat supply following a significant theft in which more than twelve tonnes of its chocolate were stolen. The incident involved a truck carrying 413,793 bars from a cen...

item.title: Diepsloot Student Earns Honours Degree, Inspiring Community
item.description: Moses Makhadai, a 24-year-old from Diepsloot, Joburg, has achieved a significant milestone by earning a Bachelor of Science Honours degree in Chemistry. This accomplishment marks him as the first pers...

item.title: Key Developments Across Africa on March 30, 2026
item.description: Today, Africa saw several major developments affecting politics, sports, and humanitarian situations. Algeria announced three days of national mourning following the death of former President Liamine ...

item.title: Hyderabad auto drivers protest by climbing cell tower amid CNG shortage and long fuel queues
item.description: In Hyderabad, tensions rose as auto-rickshaw drivers protested shortages of CNG and LPG by staging demonstrations that included climbing a nearby mobile cell tower. The incident, which occurred late o...

item.title: Arbitration fails to settle dispute over who will challenge SA featherweight champion
item.description: The ongoing dispute over who will challenge South African featherweight champion Lindelani Sibisi remains unresolved, despite a recent Boxing South Africa (BSA) arbitration ruling in favour of Bongani...

item.title: QCPD targets driver in viral gas-and-run incident for profiling
item.description: The Quezon City Police District (QCPD) announced that the driver involved in the viral 'gas-and-run' incident is now subject to profiling by authorities. The development follows intensified follow-up ...

item.title: Indira Gandhi assassin’s nephew identified after losing New Zealand court appeal in major drug trafficking case
item.description: Baltej Singh, a New Zealand-based businessman and nephew of one of Indira Gandhi’s assassins, has been publicly identified following a New Zealand court ruling that lifted a previous order suppressing...
